title: Netus

{{Quote|…we're a Republic! We have our differences, yes, but we are united in a common government!|Netus|<ref name="audio">[[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Netus''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Politics/Legends|politician]] who served in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] government during the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. After a five-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] term in the [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|Republic rocket-jumper]] corps, Netus devoted his life to politics, successfully running for a [[Senator/Legends|position]] within the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. By [[3998 BBY]], he had been appointed to the Republic's lofty position of [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]]. That year, Netus received a message from [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], who had requested [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] aid in a [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|struggle]] with the [[Naddist]] [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]]. Netus called for a meeting of the [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Committee]] to determine whether or not to send in their forces, and the group was swayed into action after a speech by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Thon]]. [[3996 BBY|Two years later]], Netus and the [[Sidrona|Supreme Chancellor]] of the Republic met with a group of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] headed by Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to discuss recent raids on Republic [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]]. After it was discovered that Ulic Qel-Droma was behind the attacks, a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] led by Qel-Droma [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|attacked]] the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. Although most of the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] had been massed at [[Kemplex Nine]] to defend against a raid that turned out to be a ruse, Netus and the Jedi were able to fight off the attack and capture Qel-Droma.

==Biography==
===Problems with the Naddists===
{{Quote|My fellow Senators… the situation on Onderon is deteriorating. We have been asked for ''assistance''. I suggest that the ''Republic fleet'' move at once.|Netus during the Defense Committee meeting|<ref name="nadd" />}}
[[File:DefenseMinistry.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Netus heads the Defense Committee's meeting.]]
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Netus served in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Rocket-Jumper Elite Advance Unit|rocket-jumper]] corps. After serving a five-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] term in the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]],<ref name="companion">''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''</ref> Netus chose a career in [[Politics/Legends|politics]]. He successfully ran for the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] and rose to the position of [[Minister of Defense (Republic)|Minister of Defense]] by the year [[3998 BBY]]. That year, a troubling issue on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]] came to Netus's attention: a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]] known as the [[Naddist]]s, led by Onderonian [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ommin/Legends|Ommin]], had [[Freedon Nadd Uprising|attacked]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] forces on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. Soon after the initial [[Funeral of Amanoa|battle]] with the Naddists, Netus received a garbled transmission from one of the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] serving on Onderon, [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. In the message, Qel-Droma detailed the events of the uprising, including the capture of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Arca Jeth]], and asked the Republic for military aid. Netus was alarmed to the point that he called a meeting of the Republic's [[Ministry of Defense|Defense Committee]] to discuss what course of action they should take.<ref name="audio" />

While Netus suggested that the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] move out at once to assist the Jedi, several [[Senator/Legends|senators]], among them the [[Unidentified senator (Orron III)|representative]] from [[Orron III]], disagreed, saying that it was a matter that the Jedi should address themselves. Suddenly, the [[Tchuukthai]] Jedi Master [[Thon]], accompanied by his [[Padawan/Legends|apprentice]], [[Nomi Sunrider]], entered the meeting chambers. Netus felt honored by the Jedi Master's presence, and with Sunrider serving as a translator, Thon admonished the assembled senators for not being truly united with the Jedi. The Tchuukthai warned the Committee that the battle against the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] did not solely concern the Jedi, but the Republic and [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] as a whole. Having made his point, Thon left the room without another word alongside Sunrider. When Netus decided to take the matter to a vote, the senators unanimously motioned in favor of aiding the Jedi.<ref name="audio" />

===The Battle of Coruscant===
{{Quote|Summon our defenses… whatever we've got left!|Netus, during the Battle of Coruscant|<ref name="thesithwar" />}}
[[File:BadassNetus.jpg|thumb|right|230px|Netus fighting in the First Battle of Coruscant in 3996 BBY]]
[[3996 BBY|Two years later]], Netus still held the position of Minister of Defense and served under the Republic's [[Sidrona|mollusk-like]] [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]]. After reports that a rogue Jedi Knight had committed [[Terrorist/Legends|terror]] attacks on Republic [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]], the [[Krevaaki]] Jedi Master [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] and a contingent of Knights—Nomi Sunrider, [[Cay Qel-Droma]], [[Dace Diath]], the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Tott Doneeta]], the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Sylvar]],<ref name="thesithwar" /> [[Nazzar/Legends|Nazzar]] [[Prince/Legends|prince]]<ref name="nadd2">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' 2]]</ref> [[Qrrrl Toq]], and the [[Miraluka/Legends|Miraluka]] [[Shoaneb Culu]]—came to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to convince the Senate to allow the Jedi to handle the crisis. Netus and the Chancellor met the group as they landed. However, as they were greeting the Jedi, Netus was brought a message from Republic Navy [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Orley Vanicus]], which revealed that Ulic Qel-Droma led the raids. Netus ordered the Republic [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to gather at [[Kemplex Nine]] in the hopes of running the enemy down, as they had received word that it was to be the next target for Ulic's raiders.<ref name="thesithwar" />

The group, especially Ulic's brother Cay, was stunned, but as they discussed these developments a large attack force appeared out of the [[Atmosphere/Legends|sky]]. At that moment, Ulic's plan was revealed: Kemplex Nine was never meant to be his next target; it was merely a ruse to draw the Republic's defenses away from their [[Capital/Legends|capital]], Coruscant. As the attacking fleet began [[Bomber/Legends|bombing]] the Coruscant streets, Netus ran for cover and ordered his subordinates to summon whatever defenses they had left to [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Sith War)|defend]] the planet. More of Qel-Droma's forces landed in the middle of [[Galactic City/Legends|Galactic City]], and the Jedi team was pressed into action. Netus was able to rally a defense force of nominal size, and the Minister of Defense, brandishing a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], joined the fight. Although Qel-Droma and his forces fought their way into the Republic's [[Republic command center|War Room]], Netus and the Jedi were able to [[Wall of light|apprehend]] him and ultimately repel the invasion.<ref name="thesithwar" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Master Thon. The presence of a Jedi Master in the capital city honors us.|Netus|<ref name="audio" />}}
As the Minister of Defense, Netus was very hard-working and spent the majority of his [[Time/Legends|time]] reviewing requests for military aid, higher budgets, new weapons, and the like, while overseeing matters of Republic security and the status of the military. Netus approached most issues with caution, but also knew that prompt action was often successful. As a result, Netus was forced to spend a large amount of time arguing for the approval of military action.<ref name="companion" /> Although Netus was an important government figure as the Minister of Defense, the former Republic rocket-jumper still was not afraid to take matters into his own hands, producing a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol and actively fighting in Ulic Qel-Droma's invasion of Coruscant.<ref name="thesithwar" /> Netus respected the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and felt that the Republic should repay them for their service in any way they could. He also held a reverence for Jedi Master Thon. Netus believed strongly in the Republic's political system and expressed to Thon that a united government could overcome the personal differences of individual politicians.<ref name="audio" /> Although Netus was [[red]]-haired in 3998 BBY,<ref name="nadd" /> by 3996 BBY he had changed his hair [[Color/Legends|color]] to [[black]]. He also had a large facial scar running from the top of his forehead to his right cheek.<ref name="thesithwar" />

==Behind the scenes==
The character of Netus first appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising|Tales of the Jedi: The Freedon Nadd Uprising]]'', the third story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Tom Veitch]] and released in [[1994]].<ref name="nadd" /> He later appeared in ''[[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War]]'', written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]].<ref name="thesithwar" /> Netus made his initial appearance in one panel of ''The Freedon Nadd Uprising''{{'s}} [[Tales of the Jedi – The Freedon Nadd Uprising 1|first issue]],<ref name="nadd" /> and in [[1995]] he appeared in ''The Sith War''{{'s}} [[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|second issue]].<ref name="thesithwar" /> In ''The Freedon Nadd Uprising'' and ''The Sith War'', Netus was illustrated by [[Tony Akins]] and [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]], respectively.<ref name="nadd" /><ref name="thesithwar" /> Netus was also featured in the [[Tales of the Jedi (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi'' audio adaptation]].<ref name="audio" /> Further information on Netus's background was provided in [[George R. Strayton]]'s [[1996]] ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement ''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''.<ref name="companion" /> Netus also garnered entries in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="swe">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="cswe">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and he was mentioned in the [[2009]] sourcebook ''[[Galaxy at War]]''.<ref name="gaw">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>
